title: Movie Date

shortDialogue.speaker: 정석
shortDialogue.english: Min-a, what kind of movie do you like?

longDialogue.speaker: 정석
longDialogue.english: Min-a, what kind of movie do you like?

longDialogue.speaker: 정석
longDialogue.english: I really like action movies, too.

longDialogue.speaker: 민아
longDialogue.korean: 재미있는 액션 영화 하나 개봉했어요. 우리 그거 볼래요?
longDialogue.english: An interesting action movie was released. Do you want to watch that?

longDialogue.speaker: 정석
longDialogue.korean: 네. 좋아요. 그럼 제가 지금 예매할게요.
longDialogue.english: Yes. Sounds good. Then, I will reserve the tickets now.

longDialogue.speaker: 정석
longDialogue.korean: 그럼 영화 보기 전에 밥 먹을까요?
longDialogue.english: Then, shall we eat before watching the movie?

longDialogue.speaker: 민아
longDialogue.korean: 네. 뭐 먹을까요? 정석 씨 좋아하는 삼겹살 먹을까요?
longDialogue.english: Ok. What shall we eat? Shall we eat pork belly, which you like?

longDialogue.speaker: 정석
longDialogue.korean: 삼겹살 먹고 싶은데 냄새날 것 같아요.
longDialogue.english: I want to eat pork belly, but I think we might smell bad.

longDialogue.speaker: 민아
longDialogue.english: Oh, then what shall we eat?

culturalTip: A typical date in Korea includes going to see a movie, but a movie date can feel different since most people genuinely enjoy seeing a movie. In fact, people love going to the movies so much that the most popular movie of the year will often draw in 10 million or more viewers, which is about a quarter of the Korean population. The price of a ticket will vary as there are multiple options to consider (which theater, 3D, 4D, Standard, Lounge Seating, VIP, etc.), but the average price is about 10,000KRW.
koreanReview:
- Short Dialogue
정석: 민아 씨, 어떤 영화 좋아해요?
민아: 저는 액션 영화 좋아해요.

- Long Dialogue
정석: 민아 씨, 어떤 영화 좋아해요?
민아: 저는 액션 영화 좋아해요.
정석: 저도 액션 영화 정말 좋아해요.
민아: 재미있는 액션 영화 하나 개봉했어요. 우리 그거 볼래요?
정석: 네. 좋아요. 그럼 제가 지금 예매할게요.
민아: 네. 고마워요.
정석: 그럼 영화 보기 전에 밥 먹을까요?
민아: 네. 뭐 먹을까요? 정석 씨 좋아하는 삼겹살 먹을까요?
정석: 삼겹살 먹고 싶은데 냄새날 것 같아요.
민아: 아, 그럼 뭐 먹을까요?
정석: 햄버거 어때요?
민아: 좋아요. 햄버거 먹으러 가요.

coreVocab.meaning: honorific suffix which implies respect toward the person whose name it is attached to

extendedVocab.meaning: to be interesting, to be fun

grammar.title: Future Tense with "Will"
grammar.meaning: Asking if someone will do something

grammar.title: Making a Suggestion to Include Self + Verb
grammar.meaning: Suggesting to go do something together

pronunciation.rule: When a syllable ends with ㄱ and is followed by a syllable that begins with a vowel, ㄱ becomes part of the following syllable rather than being pronounced distinctly as two different syllables.
When a syllable ends with ㅂ and is followed by a syllable that begins with ㅅ, the ㅅ is pronounced as ㅆ.
